comments.author: @WhereonEarth4K
comments.text:
👇 Everything we curated for this Ukraine episode. Books, history, the full cookbook, cultural resources.

*📚 BROWSE THE UKRAINE COLLECTION*
  ◦ Every book, cookbook, and cultural item we'd recommend for this episode → https://amzn.to/4wOPFum

*🛍 SEE OUR FULL W.O.E. STOREFRONT*
  ◦ Every destination Collection in one place → https://www.amazon.com/shop/whereonearth

*🎟 TOURS AND EXPERIENCES IN UKRAINE*
  ◦ Kyiv city tours and Chernobyl exclusion zone experiences → https://shorturl.at/L4Ct6
  ◦ Browse all Ukraine experiences → https://shorturl.at/EJfZb

*📖 PLAN YOUR UKRAINE TRIP*
  ◦ Ukraine Travel Guide 2026 by Ruben M. Hayes → https://amzn.to/3PAb75M
  ◦ Lonely Planet Ukrainian Phrasebook and Dictionary → https://amzn.to/4dY9t63

*📚 GO DEEPER INTO UKRAINE*
  ◦ The Gates of Europe: A History of Ukraine by Serhii Plokhy — NYT bestseller, the definitive history → https://amzn.to/4u5QZX8
  ◦ Red Famine: Stalin's War on Ukraine by Anne Applebaum — Pulitzer Prize-winning author, essential account of the Holodomor → https://amzn.to/4dUlzx6

*🍲 BRING UKRAINE HOME*
  ◦ The Authentic Ukrainian Kitchen by Yevhen Klopotenko — recipes from Ukraine's most celebrated native chef → https://amzn.to/4u5lmx6
  ◦ Kobzar (Gift Edition) by Taras Shevchenko — the foundational text of Ukrainian identity, illustrated with Shevchenko's own artwork → https://amzn.to/4vDvuhR

🌍 Every Where on Earth journey → https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLNAt9bkbci0wjQ1DY7mwpUUbHqc-I7Yro

🔔 Subscribe → https://www.youtube.com/@WhereOnEarth?sub_confirmation=1

comments.author: @eakzit3181
comments.text:
The reason Ukrainians ask to name the city on the river properly as Kyiv not Kiev - is because Kiev is russian version that unfortunately made its way into international books long before Ukrainian indepence. Kiev is the name as a reminder of hundred of years of oppression and russification (basic destruction of local customs, traditions and language substituting them with "proper language" - russian).
Its hard to explain to ppl that speak only english but I will try my best - most of slavic languages have shapes/tones(grammatical case) of the words - that changes mostly the ending of the word, sometimes trhe word completely, also ending change depending on different conditions. Back to  the history of Kyiv  Basically there is a common legend of how the name of the city was born about 3 brothers and a sister....and they named the city by the name of the eldest - Kiy.   So it was City of Kiy, took away the "City" and left "of Kiy"...the way to say  it in slavic languages is to change the ending/shape/tone/gramatical case - in Ukrainian "of Kiy" - "Kyiv", in russian it will be "Kiev". I understand that we cannot dictate how english speakers say their words - every language has different naming for the countries and cities (ask Polish people how they call Italy :)...but if you wouldnt mind please say Kyiv, not Kiev. We as Ukrainians would really appreciate it.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: That's a genuinely fascinating bit of linguistics — the case-ending shift explains a lot about why the two spellings diverged the way they did. Appreciate the detailed breakdown, and noted on Kyiv.

comments.author: @annett_annett
comments.text:
Thanks for the video

Here are some facts about Ukraine, ukrainians and our language

- British royal family members are confirmed to be Yaroslav the Wise's descendants. B. Obama is speculated to be one too

- Shchedryk/Carol of the bells was both made and adapted into English by ethnic ukrainians (Mykola Leontovych and Peter Wilhousky). It took Mykola more than 15 y. to compose it and this song was basically the last hope for our culture to survive under muscovian oppression

- cossachkas - ancient female warriors/amazons, as legends say, were the ancestors of our male warriors, cossacks + under their reign, there was the longest peace throughout UA history, for 600+- years

- hopak is one of the hardest dances

- Anton Chekhov, Mykola Hohol, Mykhaylo Bulhakov, Illya Repin, Ivan Aivazovs'kyi, Kazymyr Malevych, Arhyp Kuinji, Oleksandra Exter, Volodymyr Tatlin are famous etnic ukr writers and artists moscovia is still trying to steal from us

- Freud Sigmund, Pyotr Chaikowski, Leonardo di Caprio, Mila Kunis, Olha Kyrylenko, Milla Jovovich, Katheryn Winnick are ethnic ukrainians

- 2 strongest men on the Earth in 20th century (Ivan Piddubnyi and Ivan Firtsak) came from here. The strongest woman now is Olha Lyashchuk

- 3 world's successful boxers are ukrainians: Klychko brothers and Oleksandr Usyk

- the biggest amount of monuments in the world is dedicated to T. Shevchenko - 1350+

- UA is one of the most successful countries in ESC. We are the only ones to be qualified for finals every time we parttake in the contest and we took the 1st place the 2nd year right after our journey in eurovision began. 3 wins in total, 2 2nd places, 2 3rd places, 15 times among 21 in top10

- Diia, the 1st app for digitalised documents was made and launched in UA

- Nova Poshta (the biggest post network here) is acknowledged the best in the world

- the Internet in Ukraine is one of the fastest, most stable and affordable

- Ukraine's the biggest oil and the 3rd largest honey exporter/manufacturer. Btw, a frame for beehives was created by Petro Prokopovych. Until then, bees were killed just so humanity could have access to honey

- sushi are the most popular (and probably the tastiest) in UA after Japan

- ukr language is one of the richest and oldest slavic languages, one of the richest in synonyms in general and one of the hardest in the world to learn. It's even said it can describe indescribable phenomena. This language is also the most banned ever. 134 times it was canceled, oppressed, "rewritten", forbidden to speak/study in/publish in/sing in/be used at work, assimilated to muscovian and it still survived. In 2022 it was the fastest-growing language course globally. Also, the largest amount of folk songs are in ukrainian (500k+-)

- trembita is the largest musical instrument

- Khreshchatyk is the widest+shortest central street in EU

- UA has the longest trolleybus route (Crimea, 80+ km) and the longest promenade in EU (Dripro, 25+ km)

- the largest square in EU is in Kharkiv

- ukrainian's the 2nd language "Animal farm" by G. Orwell was translated into. He even funded the translation himself and wrote a special introductory preface explicitly for ukrainians

- the deepest in EU and 2nd deepest railway station is Arsenal'na, Kyiv

- Kyiv used to be 10 times larger than Paris and 50 times bigger than London

- the 1st school for girls was founded in Kyiv, in 1086

- McD in Kyiv is/used to be the 3rd most visited in the world

- the 1st coffee shop in EU was opened by an etnic Ukrainian, Yurii Kulchyts'kyi, who also was the one to come up with the idea of adding milk and sugar to coffee

- the 1st female officer and the 1st one to solve the 400-y.-o. math problem were ukr women

- Sich is considered to be one of the 1st democratic states in the world

- the oldest local/landscape map was found here

- EU's geographical center is in UA

- P. Picasso admired Kateryna Bilokur's art

- UA has 25% of world's chornOzem

- 4 of 12 lavras are in UA

- only Ukraine's coat of arms is depicted in the St. Peter and Paul Cathedral in the Vatican

- a ukrainian once saved W. Churchill from execution

- G. Gershwin wrote "Summertime" after hearing a ukr lullaby “Ой ходить сон біля вікон”

- the Motherland statue is bigger than Jesus the redeemer and the Liberty statue

Німецькі нацисти намагалися знищити єврейську націю, а російські нацисти намагаються знищити українську націю. Вони викрадають українських дітей, щоб перетворити їх на росіян...

comments.text:
Wow! What a fascinating story about the past and present of Ukraine. There were so many facts that even I, as a native Ukrainian, didn't know. It's a shame that subtitles and alternative language audio options aren't available, because I would gladly share this video with many more people.

Thank you for creating this. It's more than a documentary—it's a remarkable journey through history and culture. Great work!
🇺🇲🇺🇦
